ocr.text: As the Asia-Pacific region continues to prioritize renewable energy solutions, understanding the evolving landscape of battery
storage costs is essential for stakeholders in the energy sector. The accompanying data table illustrates the projected cost curves
for battery storage from 2023 to 2028, with a particular focus on the TotalCost metric, which highlights the overall financial
implications of these technologies. For instance, in 2023, the TotalCost for battery storage was estimated at 480 HKD, reflecting
the initial investments required to enhance energy storage capabilities.
CostMetrics  PerformanceRatios  Forecast
UnitCost(HKD/kWh)  CapacityFactor(%)  CycleLife(Cycles)  Efficiency(%)  InvestmentIndex
As we look ahead, this cost is projected to rise slightly to 530 HKD by 2028, indicating a gradual increase in the economic
burden associated with these systems. Notably, the 2027 projections reveal that the cost curve is expected to stabilize, with
values of 0.90 HKD and 0.94 HKD representing the anticipated unit costs for that year. This data emphasizes the importance of
strategic planning and investment in battery storage technologies, particularly as the region aims to meet ambitious
sustainability targets. Furthermore, the growing reliance on renewable energy sources necessitates a closer examination of these
cost trajectories, with an average TotalCost of 520 HKD across the projected timeline. By understanding these trends,
stakeholders can better navigate the financial landscape of energy storage solutions and make informed decisions that support
the region's transition to a more sustainable energy future.
